text: GNC = Guidance, Navigation, and Control. GNC usually means Guidance, Navigation, and Control in aerospace flight-system contexts.
EDL = Entry, Descent, and Landing. EDL usually means Entry, Descent, and Landing for planetary mission phases.
LEO = Low Earth Orbit. LEO usually means Low Earth Orbit; exact altitude bounds are authority/project dependent.
MEO = Medium Earth Orbit. MEO usually means Medium Earth Orbit; exact altitude bounds are authority/project dependent.
GEO = Geostationary Earth Orbit. GEO usually means Geostationary Earth Orbit in spacecraft mission-design contexts.
PDR = Preliminary Design Review. PDR usually means Preliminary Design Review in aerospace systems-engineering review contexts.
CDR = Critical Design Review. CDR often means Critical Design Review in program lifecycle contexts, but the token is ambiguous.
CDR = Command/Data Recorder. CDR can refer to a recorder/data-system meaning in avionics contexts; do not assume design review.
TRR = Test Readiness Review. TRR usually means Test Readiness Review in test program contexts.
TRL = Technology Readiness Level. TRL usually means Technology Readiness Level; scale semantics depend on the governing authority.
RCS = Reaction Control System. RCS can mean Reaction Control System in spacecraft propulsion/attitude-control contexts.
RCS = Radar Cross Section. RCS can mean Radar Cross Section in radar/electromagnetic-signature contexts.
AC = Advisory Circular. AC can mean Advisory Circular in FAA/regulatory contexts; the token is ambiguous.
AC = Alternating Current. AC can mean Alternating Current in electrical power contexts.
ADS-B = Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast. ADS-B usually means Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast in aviation surveillance contexts.
BLSS = Bioregenerative Life Support System. BLSS appears throughout the current repository in life-support and thin-film/biofilm contexts.
ECLSS = Environmental Control and Life Support System. ECLSS appears in current validation/source-registry and life-support contexts.
BVAD = Baseline Values and Assumptions Document. BVAD appears in current source references and must remain source-scoped.
MELiSSA = Micro-Ecological Life Support System Alternative. MELiSSA is mixed-case; exact source spelling should be preserved.
PBR = Photobioreactor. PBR appears in current thin-film and life-support source material.
PDE = Partial Differential Equation. PDE appears in current thin-film model documentation.
ROM = Reduced-Order Model. ROM appears in current thin-film model documentation.
CEA = Chemical Equilibrium with Applications. CEA appears as a source/tool name in the current repository and dependency policy.
NACA = National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics. NACA appears in current source-registry and gas-dynamics contexts.
JPL = Jet Propulsion Laboratory. JPL appears in current astrodynamics source-registry contexts.
NIST = National Institute of Standards and Technology. NIST appears in current constants source-registry contexts.
CODATA = Committee on Data for Science and Technology. CODATA appears in current constants source-registry contexts.
DOI = Digital Object Identifier. DOI appears in current citation and bibliography source material.
SI = International System of Units. SI appears in current unit and validation documentation.
ISS = International Space Station. ISS appears in current source material and life-support references.
API = Application Programming Interface. API appears in current documentation and generated API summaries.
CI = Continuous Integration. CI appears in repository governance and workflow contexts.
CSV = Comma-Separated Values. CSV appears in current data manifests and source verification files.
JSON = JavaScript Object Notation. JSON appears in current schemas, manifests, and generated indexes.
YAML = YAML Ain't Markup Language. YAML appears in current validation and nomenclature registry contexts.
NASA = National Aeronautics and Space Administration. NASA is used as a source authority and agency label in current AeroCodex materials.
FAA = Federal Aviation Administration. FAA is used as a source authority and aviation context marker.
ECSS = European Cooperation for Space Standardization. ECSS is used as a standards authority and glossary source family.
CCSDS = Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems. CCSDS is used as a standards authority for space data and communications.
AIAA = American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics. AIAA is used as a publication and nomenclature-guidance source authority.
DOD = Department of Defense. DOD/DoD is used as a defense terminology source authority.
ESM = Equivalent System Mass. ESM appears in current life-support and thin-film trade-study code.
TIC = Total Inorganic Carbon. TIC appears in current photobioreactor carbonate-chemistry code.
NAN = Not a Number. NAN appears as Rust floating-point NaN handling in current code.
Aircraft Registration: Civil registration identifier assigned to an aircraft.
Sample Count: Number of samples in a collection, window, or buffer when the collection context is explicitly declared.
Node Count: Number of nodes in a graph, tree, network, or topology object.
Sample Interval: Time interval between adjacent samples in a regularly sampled sequence.
Ground Speed: Magnitude of horizontal velocity relative to the ground.
Station: Ambiguous term requiring context. Do not use as a canonical field without qualifier.
Guidance, Navigation, and Control: Integrated flight-system functions that estimate state, plan or follow guidance objectives, and command control actions.
Entry, Descent, and Landing: Mission phase spanning atmospheric entry, controlled descent, and landing or touchdown operations.
Low Earth Orbit: Earth-orbit regime commonly called LEO; exact altitude bounds must be supplied by the governing source or project.
Medium Earth Orbit: Earth-orbit regime commonly called MEO; exact altitude bounds must be supplied by the governing source or project.
Geostationary Earth Orbit: Earth orbit commonly called GEO in mission-design and communications-satellite contexts.
Preliminary Design Review: Program lifecycle review commonly used to assess whether a preliminary design is ready to proceed toward detailed design.
Critical Design Review: Program lifecycle review commonly used to assess whether a detailed design is mature enough to proceed toward fabrication, integration, or implementation.
Command/Data Recorder: Candidate avionics or spacecraft data-system meaning for CDR; keep project-local unless approved by a governing glossary.
Test Readiness Review: Review commonly used to assess whether test objectives, configuration, procedures, resources, and risks are ready for execution.
Technology Readiness Level: Readiness or maturity level assigned to a technology under a governing scale.
Reaction Control System: Spacecraft subsystem or function that uses control effectors such as thrusters for attitude or small translational control.
Radar Cross Section: Radar-signature quantity or model term associated with electromagnetic scattering from a target.
Advisory Circular: FAA-facing source term for an advisory circular; use only when the governing source context is FAA or aviation regulation.
Alternating Current: Electrical current whose direction or polarity varies periodically; use in electrical-power contexts.
Automatic Dependent Surveillance-Broadcast: Aviation surveillance concept commonly abbreviated ADS-B; verify operational meaning against applicable FAA or aviation source.
Bioregenerative Life Support System: Life-support system concept using biological processes to close or reduce consumable loops.
Environmental Control and Life Support System: Habitat or spacecraft system domain concerned with atmosphere, water, waste, thermal, and crew-support functions.
Baseline Values and Assumptions Document: Project or agency reference document containing baseline values, assumptions, and planning factors.
Micro-Ecological Life Support System Alternative: Life-support research program or architecture family referred to as MELiSSA in source material.
Photobioreactor: Reactor or cultivation system using light-driven biological production.
Partial Differential Equation: Equation involving derivatives of one or more dependent variables with respect to multiple independent variables.
Reduced-Order Model: Simplified model intended to approximate a higher-fidelity model with fewer states, modes, equations, or computational cost.
Chemical Equilibrium with Applications: NASA chemical-equilibrium analysis program family commonly referred to as CEA in thermodynamics and propulsion contexts.
National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics: Historical aeronautics authority whose reports and airfoil data are commonly cited in aerospace engineering.
Jet Propulsion Laboratory: NASA-affiliated laboratory and source authority frequently cited for astrodynamics and spacecraft materials.
International Space Station: Crewed orbital facility used as a spacecraft and life-support reference context.
Digital Object Identifier: Persistent identifier used for scholarly and technical source references.
International System of Units: International measurement-unit system used for SI units and unit symbols.
National Institute of Standards and Technology: United States standards institute and source authority for physical constants and measurement references.
Committee on Data for Science and Technology: International scientific data committee acronym used in CODATA physical constants references.
Application Programming Interface: Software interface exposed by a crate, module, service, or tool.
Continuous Integration: Software practice and automation context for running checks on repository changes.
Comma-Separated Values: Plain-text tabular data format used in manifests and source maps.
JavaScript Object Notation: Machine-readable data serialization format used in manifests and generated indexes.
YAML Ain't Markup Language: Human-readable data serialization format used for registry and validation-card files.
AeroCodex xtask: Repository-local Rust task runner used for validation scaffold checks.
National Aeronautics and Space Administration: United States civil space and aeronautics agency frequently used as a source authority.
Federal Aviation Administration: United States civil aviation agency and regulatory/source authority.
European Cooperation for Space Standardization: European space standards cooperation and standards source authority.
Consultative Committee for Space Data Systems: International space data and communications standards body/source authority.
American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics: Aerospace professional society and publication/source-guidance authority.
Department of Defense: Defense source authority for terminology, acquisition, range, and space-operation contexts.
Equivalent System Mass: Systems-analysis metric that converts mass, volume, power, cooling, and crew-time penalties into a common mass-equivalent basis.
Total Inorganic Carbon: Carbonate-system concentration or rate variable used in water, bioreactor, and life-support chemistry contexts.
Not a Number: Floating-point value category representing undefined or unrepresentable numeric results.
tail_number -> aircraft_registration
n_number -> aircraft_registration
registration_mark -> aircraft_registration
dt -> sample_interval
delta_t -> sample_interval
station -> ['airport_station', 'maintenance_station', 'fuselage_station', 'reporting_station']
gs -> ground_speed
GNC -> guidance_navigation_and_control
GN&C -> guidance_navigation_and_control
EDL -> entry_descent_and_landing
LEO -> low_earth_orbit
MEO -> medium_earth_orbit
GEO -> geostationary_earth_orbit
PDR -> preliminary_design_review
CDR -> ['critical_design_review', 'command_data_recorder']
TRR -> test_readiness_review
TRL -> technology_readiness_level
RCS -> ['reaction_control_system', 'radar_cross_section']
AC -> ['advisory_circular', 'alternating_current']
ADS-B -> automatic_dependent_surveillance_broadcast
